name: Warren Jeffs
role_title: President/Prophet, Fundamentalist Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints (FLDS)
charges: Aggravated sexual assault of a child (12-year-old 'spiritual bride'), sexual assault of a child (15-year-old 'spiritual bride') — arranged polygamous child marriages; was on FBI's Ten Most Wanted list
outcome: Life + 20 years; $10,000 fine
source1_description: Texas Department of Criminal Justice inmate record
source2_description: FBI Ten Most Wanted case file

name: Tony Alamo (Bernie Lazar Hoffman)
role_title: Founder and leader, Tony Alamo Christian Ministries, Fouke Arkansas
charges: 10 counts transporting minors across state lines for sexual purposes — took girls (including a 9-year-old) as 'wives' in secret ceremonies; five women testified they were 'married' to him as minors (died in federal prison May 2, 2017 at age 82)
outcome: 175 years (died in custody May 2, 2017)
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Alamo conviction

name: Jim Bakker
role_title: Television evangelist, co-founder, PTL Ministries ('Praise the Lord')
charges: 24 counts wire and mail fraud, conspiracy — defrauded followers who purchased 'lifetime partnerships' at Heritage USA resort; misappropriated $3.7 million+ for personal use
outcome: Sentence reduced to 18 years on appeal; paroled 1994 after 5 years; $500,000 fine
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Bakker conviction

name: Richard Dortch
role_title: Co-host, PTL television ministry; associate of Jim Bakker
charges: Wire fraud, mail fraud, conspiracy — assisted Jim Bakker's PTL fraud scheme (cooperating witness against Bakker)
outcome: 8 years reduced; $200,000 fine; released after ~2 years
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Dortch conviction

name: Kirbyjon H. Caldwell
role_title: Senior Pastor, Windsor Village United Methodist Church, Houston TX (~14,000 members); former spiritual adviser to Presidents George W. Bush and Barack Obama
charges: Conspiracy to commit wire fraud — sold worthless historical Chinese bonds to investors and pocketed approximately $3.5 million from elderly investors
outcome: 6 years in federal prison; $3.6 million restitution
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Caldwell sentencing

name: Larry Ray (born Lawrence Grecco)
role_title: Cult leader, 'Sarah Lawrence Sex Cult' — moved into daughter's college dormitory and recruited victims
charges: Sex trafficking, extortion, forced labor, racketeering conspiracy, money laundering, tax evasion — psychologically, physically, and sexually abused Sarah Lawrence College students for nearly a decade; coerced victims into prostitution
outcome: 60 years + lifetime supervised release; $2.4 million forfeiture
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Larry Ray sentencing
source2_description: New York Times Sarah Lawrence cult coverage

name: Lori Vallow Daybell
role_title: Member, doomsday cult ('Preparing a People'); wife of self-styled religious author Chad Daybell
charges: First-degree murder of children Joshua 'JJ' Vallow (age 7) and Tylee Ryan (age 16); conspiracy to commit murder of Chad Daybell's first wife Tammy Daybell; additional murder and attempted murder charges in Arizona — killed own children based on cult belief they were 'zombies'
outcome: Three consecutive life terms without parole, Idaho + two additional life sentences, Arizona
conviction_date: 2023 (Idaho convicted May; sentenced Jul. 31); 2025 (Arizona conviction July)

name: Chad Daybell
role_title: Self-styled religious author and doomsday cult leader ('Preparing a People')
charges: First-degree murder of his first wife Tammy Daybell and of Lori Vallow's two children (JJ Vallow and Tylee Ryan); conspiracy charges — buried children's bodies on his property; murders motivated by apocalyptic religious beliefs
outcome: Death penalty

name: Lamor Whitehead
role_title: Pastor / church leader, Leaders of Tomorrow International Ministries, Brooklyn NY
charges: Wire fraud, attempted wire fraud, attempted extortion, making false statements to federal law enforcement — stole millions in financial frauds including stealing from a parishioner
outcome: 9 years in federal prison; $85,000 restitution; $95,000 forfeiture
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Whitehead sentencing

name: James Arthur Ray
role_title: Self-help/spiritual speaker and motivational author; led 'Spiritual Warrior' retreat, Sedona AZ (endorsed by Oprah Winfrey; charged $9,000+ per person; died Jan. 3, 2025)
charges: Three counts negligent homicide — three participants died in an improperly run, dangerously overcrowded and overheated sweat lodge ceremony during a five-day retreat
outcome: 2 years in Arizona state prison; released Jul. 12, 2013 (died January 3, 2025)

name: Father Gilbert Gauthe
role_title: Roman Catholic priest, Diocese of Lafayette, Louisiana
charges: Aggravated rape, crimes against nature, child pornography, contributing to the delinquency of a minor — sexually abused at least 37 children over a decade (first Catholic priest in the U.S. to face a widely publicized criminal trial for child sexual abuse)
outcome: 20 years at hard labor, no parole; released 1995

name: Father John Geoghan
role_title: Roman Catholic priest, Archdiocese of Boston MA
charges: Indecent assault and battery on a child — central figure in the Boston Globe 'Spotlight' investigation; accused by over 130 people; archdiocese transferred him repeatedly (killed by fellow inmate Aug. 23, 2003 while appeal was pending)
outcome: 9 to 10 years; killed in prison 2003

name: Father Paul Shanley
role_title: Roman Catholic priest, Archdiocese of Boston MA
charges: Child rape, indecent assault and battery on a child — repeatedly raped a boy over several years beginning when the child was approximately 6 years old (archdiocese covered up allegations for decades; laicized 2004; died Oct. 28, 2020)
outcome: 12 to 15 years; released Jul. 2017 (died Oct. 28, 2020)

name: Bishop Robert W. Finn
role_title: Bishop of Kansas City-St. Joseph diocese, Missouri
charges: Failure to report suspected child abuse — failed to promptly inform authorities about a priest (Father Shawn Ratigan) connected to child pornography (became first sitting U.S. bishop convicted of a criminal offense for mishandling abuse complaints; resigned April 2015)
outcome: 2 years probation suspended; $10,000 counseling fund

name: Monsignor William Lynn
role_title: Secretary for Clergy, Archdiocese of Philadelphia — supervised hundreds of priests
charges: Child endangerment — covered up known sex abuse complaints against priests; transferred predator Father Edward Avery to a new parish where abuse continued (first U.S. Catholic official convicted of a crime for shielding abusive priests)
outcome: 3 to 6 years

name: Bishop Gustavo Zanchetta
role_title: Bishop of Orán, Argentina; later appointed to Vatican financial post (APSA) by Pope Francis
charges: Sexual abuse of adult seminarians — inappropriate sexual advances and harassment (case raised significant questions about Pope Francis's handling of the abuse allegations; Vatican was informed of complaints as early as 2015)
outcome: 4.5 years

name: Terry L. Hall
role_title: Lead pastor, church in McLeansboro IL (Hamilton County)
charges: Wire fraud (2 counts), making false statements (3 counts) — fraudulently obtained $199,900 in COVID-19 SBA relief money in church's name; spent funds on personal mortgage, construction, travel, clothing; created fake documents and intimidated church members
outcome: 21 months in federal prison; $199,900 restitution to SBA
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Hall sentencing

name: Shin Ok-ju
role_title: Founder and leader, Grace Road Church (doomsday cult), South Korea / Fiji
charges: Violence, child abuse, fraud, illegal confinement — held 400 followers captive in Fiji, confiscated passports, subjected followers to beatings to 'drive out evil spirits'
outcome: 6 years
source2_description: BBC coverage of Grace Road cult

name: João Teixeira de Faria ('John of God')
role_title: Brazilian 'spiritual healer,' Abadiânia, Brazil — treated tens of thousands monthly at his 'spiritual hospital' (featured on Oprah Winfrey's show; over 600 women came forward as accusers)
charges: Rape, rape via fraud, rape of a vulnerable individual — sexually abused female followers across 17 consolidated cases
outcome: 118 years 6 months 15 days across 17 cases

name: Adnan Oktar
role_title: Turkish televangelist and self-styled Islamic creationist author; surrounded himself with female followers called 'kittens'
charges: Leading a criminal organization, sexual exploitation of minors, rape, extortion, unlawful confinement, political and military espionage (10 total charges)
outcome: 8,658 years on retrial

name: Dennis Finbow
role_title: Former Roman Catholic priest, Peterborough UK
charges: 15 counts of indecent assault involving five victims (two male, three female), majority under age 16; abuse 1975–1992; acknowledged additional offenses in July 2025
jurisdiction: Cambridge Crown Court, England and Wales
outcome: 11 years combined; stripped of clerical status

name: Julio César Grassi
role_title: Catholic priest, founder 'Fundación Felices los Niños' (Happy Children Foundation), Argentina (Pope John Paul II had honored him for charitable work; Argentine Supreme Court upheld conviction 2016)
charges: Sexual abuse of minors in his care at the foundation
outcome: 15 years

name: Isaiah Teague
role_title: Youth pastor, church in Meridian, Idaho
charges: Sexual abuse of a minor — groomed and sexually abused a 14-year-old girl for approximately one year while threatening her silence; abuse occurred in his home while his wife and child slept nearby
outcome: Life in prison, minimum 20 years; 50-year no-contact order with minors

name: Walter Chahwanda
role_title: Pastor, church in Liverpool UK
charges: 17 offenses including sexual offenses against young women and children
jurisdiction: Liverpool Crown Court, England and Wales
outcome: 14 years: 9 years custody + 5 years extended licence

name: George Pell (Cardinal)
role_title: Cardinal, Archbishop of Melbourne (later Vatican Secretariat of Economy) — most senior Catholic cleric ever convicted of child sex abuse at time of conviction
charges: 5 counts child sex offenses — sexually assaulted two 13-year-old choirboys at St. Patrick's Cathedral, Melbourne, in the late 1990s (High Court unanimously overturned on grounds insufficient evidence to exclude reasonable doubt; died January 10, 2023)
outcome: Conviction unanimously overturned by High Court Apr. 7, 2020; had served 404 days in solitary confinement
conviction_date: 2019 (convicted Dec. 2018; sentenced Mar. 13); conviction overturned April 7, 2020
